demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: They keep having seizures on their right side, and they won't wake up at all between the fits. (Reported by the parents — the child is 5 and is not responding.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Three or four seizures down the right side over the last hour and a half, we gave the rescue medicine but another one happened and now he just won't wake up.

symptom_narrative.duration: ongoing for about an hour and a half and not settling
symptom_narrative.character: shaking/jerking down the right side — right arm and right leg, and now his right face and arm are twitching; he is limp and floppy and won't wake up in between
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parents say this is much worse than his usual seizures and he isn't coming round like he normally does
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right side of the body — arm, leg and face
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: three or four separate seizures each lasting a few minutes, one after another, without him waking up properly between them
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — the rescue medicine we gave didn't stop them
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: very drowsy and unresponsive between the seizures, breathing looks shallow
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has had seizures since he was a baby and they are hard to control, but he usually wakes up between them and they don't come one after another like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally he can walk but with a limp on the right; right now he is completely limp and not responding

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Sturge-Weber syndrome (a condition he was born with)
past_medical_history.how_managed: followed by the children's neurology and eye clinics
daily seizure medicines plus a rescue medicine for bad seizures

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: weakness down the right side (he walks with a limp)
a reddish-purple birthmark over the left side of his forehead and eyelid, present since birth
glaucoma (high pressure) in the left eye
past_medical_history.how_managed: seen by the children's eye clinic

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: buccal midazolam rescue medicine (given into the cheek for bad seizures)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 5-year-old child, lives at home with both parents; both parents are present and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known congenital condition with right-sided weakness and a longstanding seizure disorder; usually ambulant with a right-sided limp

objective_data_offered.value: right side of the face and the right arm are visibly twitching rhythmically on camera right now
he is completely limp and floppy, not responding to us or to being touched, and his breathing looks shallow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent observation, not a measured assessment

objective_data_offered.value: a reddish-purple birthmark over the left forehead and upper eyelid
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: longstanding birthmark the parents say has been there since birth

objective_data_offered.value: the buccal midazolam rescue medicine pack is empty on the counter — used about 45 minutes ago
